text:
The Succession Contest (, Keishō-sen; also known as Succession Battle, etc.) is a generational event to determine the King of the Kakin Empire. In a manner with several parallels to Kodoku, the participants compete in a battle to the death, with the sole survivor earning the right to the throne.

Overview

The Succession Contest is a generational event, held to determine the next King of the Kakin Empire. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 348 Since only one instance of the event has been witnessed in the series (though references to another have been made), it is unclear what factors vary and what remain consistent between contests. Some factors, like identifying a suitable time and location for a particular instance of the contest, are seemingly determined by the current King of Kakin. It should be kept in mind that many statements made here about the general Succession Contest are inferred or refer solely to the one Succession Contest seen taking place within the series.

The current Succession Contest takes place on board the Black Whale, a ship intended for transport to the Dark Continent. Travel is expected to take two months, exiting humanity's normal waters around three weeks in. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 Nasubi's declaration of the contest includes the winning criteria, that "the sole survivor of [the] voyage will be the next King". 2 nd and 3 rd Princes Camilla Hui Guo Rou and Zhang Lei Hui Guo Rou independently seek clarification on its wording, but are both told that how "the sole survivor is the official heir to the throne" is interpreted is part of the contest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 When Nasubi's butler Nugui comes to 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou to inform of the contest and to conduct the Seed Urn Ceremony for him, Nugui states that "once [the contest] begins, the right to succession will not be questioned no matter what measures [one takes] to outlast the others". 

A complex and nuances set of rules determine eligibility for participation, but all 14 of Kakin's Princes meet the criteria and become participants. The Kakin Empire has three regalia, objects forming the system used to create the powerful Nen needed for both the succession battle and maintaining the prosperity of Kakin at large. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 

Previous Succession Contests

Currently, only one succession contest has been witnessed in the series, between the children of Nasubi Hui Guo Rou's legal wives to determine his successor. It has been mentioned that Nasubi himself participated in a succession contest, though nothing about it beyond the fact that was its winner is currently known. Nasubi's half-brother claims Nasubi used a strategy of "all things come to those who wait" to win, and believes he can locate more information on it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 390 

Current Succession Contest

Background

Sometime after announcing that the Kakin Empire would colonize the Dark Continent through an expedition led by Beyond Netero, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 340 Nasubi declared that the Succession Contest to determine his successor would take place on board the Black Whale 1, the ship expected to take the voyagers there. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 342 The competition began at noon on the day of the Black Whale 1's departure, after the ship finished sounding its departure horn. Until the contest began, invited participants were not informed of how many people planned to enter, and were informed that the contest would be canceled if any candidate died before the departure ceremony. All 14 potential participants attended the departure ceremony, making the contest a 14-way battle royale. 

Outcome

Since the winner of the contest is its sole survivor, participants are implied to be eligible and not have dropped out as long as they are still alive. Participants who drop out have their rooms sealed for the remainder of the voyage and phone lines cut. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 The bodies of deceased participants are placed in a mysterious burial chamber with fourteen caskets, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 though it currently remains unclear what non-ceremonial purpose this has, if any.

As a general rule of thumb, participants with both body and soul dead are considered to have dropped out. This complexity in rules is introduced because of 9 th Prince Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou's ability which can separate the soul of a Prince from their body; Halkenburg himself is one such instance of a Prince with soul and body separated. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Since the Succession Contest is currently in progress, this table summarizes the progression of the contest as of Chapter 410.

Parties

Participants

There are several criteria for participation:
Participation in the current Succession Contest is limited to only children of the legal wives of the current king of Kakin, King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 
The wording of the eligibility criteria leaves room for bastard children to participate; children to a legal wife of the current king but without the current king as their biological father. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 
It is unclear if only biological children (of legal wives) are eligible to participate, or if adoptive children are able to participate as well.
Though 9 th Prince Halkenburg is known to be an adoptive child of 2 nd Queen Duazul Hui Guo Rou, this is insufficient to conclude that adoptive children are allowed to participate, since Halkenburg also qualifies to participate by being the biological child of 1 st Queen Unma Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 
Children of mistresses or non-legal wives of the King are relegated to being second-track fakers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 
Potential participants that wish to participate undergo the Seed Urn Ceremony, part of the Kakin Royal Family tradition, said to grant participants a special power by proving their inheritance to the urn with blood and focusing on their desire for the crown. 
In actuality, this ceremony grants each participant a Guardian Spirit Beast which assists them throughout the contest. 
For the current Succession Contest, potential participants are also required to board the Black Whale and take part in its departure ceremony. 
Participants were informed that the contest would be canceled if any of them died before the contest began. 

The participants are all referred to as Princes, regardless of gender, and are described as the "n th Prince" to reflect the order of their birth. The current Succession Contest has fourteen participants, listed below in order of birth:
Benjamin Hui Guo Rou 
Camilla Hui Guo Rou 
Zhang Lei Hui Guo Rou 
Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou 
Tubeppa Hui Guo Rou 
Tyson Hui Guo Rou 
Luzurus Hui Guo Rou 
Salé-salé Hui Guo Rou (Eliminated) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382

Momoze Hui Guo Rou (Eliminated) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 
Marayam Hui Guo Rou 
Woble Hui Guo Rou 

 
|-|Benjamin Hui Guo Rou=

Benjamin Hui Guo Rou

1 st Prince Benjamin is arguably the most advantaged participant of the succession contest. Physically, he is likely one of the strongest people on the ship, and almost certainly the strongest succession contest participant by a large margin, meaning that he likely does not need to fear physical unarmed combat or threats. He is the Deputy Military Adviser of the Kakin Royal Army, allowing him to not only declare special martial law, but have freedom to move and act as he wishes (within reason) during special martial law, a privilege that other Princes do not have, who are forced to listen to instructions from the military during such time. Benjamin's personal guards are comprised of his personal elite soldiers, all official soldiers of the Royal Army, allowing Benjamin to send them to join the guard of the other Princes, who are his competitors in the succession contest. Being a son of the first Queen, he has the privilege of not having any guards sent by other Queens in his guard.

|-|Camilla Hui Guo Rou=

Camilla Hui Guo Rou

|-|Zhang Lei Hui Guo Rou=

Zhang Lei Hui Guo Rou

3 rd Prince Zhang Lei could be considered the least advantaged of the higher Princes at the very least in terms of Nen, but has arguably displayed more consideration for strategy and appreciation for information than all of his siblings; all of his actions seen thus far have been to gather more information rather than directly influence his prospects for the contest.

Zhang Lei's two older siblings entered the contest knowing Nen, and his younger half-brother Tserriednich is a generational Nen talent. Even his men know nothing of Nen, but Zhang Lei is able to somewhat mitigate this deficit by correctly guessing at the importance of Nen and making arrangements to gain information on Nen; his fortune in realizing this early playing a part in securing his partnership with Kurapika.

Zhang Lei is thoughtful and strategic; right from the start, Zhang Lei decided to take measures to learn about Nen shortly after correctly guessing its importance to the succession contest and hearing that his men know nothing of it. The amount of time Zhang Lei is seen pondering about his Guardian Spirit Beast's ability is second to none, his guesses concerning its nature being based around his perception of himself as someone who worries more about what happens after he takes the throne before he even becomes King.

Zhang Lei makes use of strategy and connections throughout the contest. He is the only of the three mafia Princes to be seen making use of their mafia connection; Tserriednich had the misfortune of the new boss of his beneficiary mafia family choosing to stage a revolt on board the Black Whale, and Luzurus has not been seen interacting with his at all. Zhang Lei uses his connection with Onior Longbao, boss of the Xi-Yu Family, to try and gain information on both Nen and his father Nasubi's succession contest.

Salé-salé Hui Guo Rou

8 th Prince Salé-salé was the second participant to drop out of the contest and would be assassinated on the sixth day Speculative but likely time. It is known that Yushohi's ability takes longer to activate on non-Nen users, and there are no signs that Salé-salé was a Nen user. Possible timing ranges from anytime on the fifth day to before dawn on the seventh day. by Benjamin's man Yushohi. He seemed generally disinterested in the contest at large, preferring to indulge himself by partying continuously with women, whom Salé-salé elected to fill his fifteen allotted employees with instead of guards in preparation for the contest.

Though Salé-salé's Guardian Spirit Beast did not display any offensive or defensive capabilities, Benjamin's man Rihan elected to eliminate it using his own ability, to create an opportunity for Salé-salé to be later assassinated by Yushohi. After managing to do so on the fourth day, Rihan would trade shifts with Yushohi, who would eventually assassinate Salé-salé. Salé-salé's absence from the Sunday banquet several days later was attributed to him being in poor health; Salé-salé had originally planned a rap performance Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385 that he believed would "change the world". 

|-|Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou=

Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou

|-|Kacho Hui Guo Rou=

Kacho Hui Guo Rou

10 th Prince Kacho Hui Guo Rou was the third participant to drop out of the succession contest and would be killed by mysterious Nen Speculated to be the workings of the Lotus Anchorite on the night of the eighth day of the voyage during an unsuccessful escape attempt. While alive, she put on a bratty facade to distance herself from those around her, but her bodyguard Melody believed this to be a means of Kacho subtly ensuring that someone forced to choose between her or her twin sister 11 th Prince Fugetsu Hui Guo Rou would choose Fugetsu. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Their plan for their escape attempt involved using Fugetsu's Magical Worm ability to teleport them back to safety. It requires Fugetsu to open the door, and during their escape, Kacho shut the door behind Fugetsu, possibly to improve the chances of Fugetsu surviving, rather than risk them both dying if Kacho tried to save herself as well. This act resulted in Kacho's death, whether because Kacho did not have sufficient time to reopen the door, or was Nen-restricted into being unable to open the door, and so died as a result. 

After Kacho's death, her Guardian Spirit Beast's ability activated. Its ability was to take the form of the twin that died, and so it assumed Kacho's form. While in this form, Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast continued to support Fugetsu, together with Melody and Kaiser from the Justice Bureau. 

|-|Fugetsu Hui Guo Rou=

Fugetsu Hui Guo Rou

|-|Momoze Hui Guo Rou=

Momoze Hui Guo Rou

12 th Prince Momoze was the first participant to drop out of the succession contest, and died only about 9 hours after the contest began on the night of the first day, at the hands of her guard Tuffdy, assigned to her by 5 th Queen Swinko-swinko. Shortly after Momoze and her brother Marayam separately retire from the departure ceremony, their mother 7 th Queen Sevanti Hui Guo Rou restructures the guards of her two children, moving most of Momoze's guards to the guard of her younger brother, and leaving Momoze with only six guards, one from each of the six Queens superior to Sevanti. 

Perhaps as a reflection of Momoze's personality, Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast is one of the few that explore Tier 1 and is seen wandering into Room 1014 by its Nen-awakened occupants. In possibly another reflection of its host's personality, Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast immediately decides to act, and interprets Sayird's statement that he is free as Sayird agreeing to its requests, then using pseudo-coercive manipulation to force Sayird into killing three of Woble's then ten-remaining guards. 

As a result of this, Momoze feels unnaturally fatigued, though she herself is unaware of it. As Momoze sleeps, her Guardian Spirit Beast slowly fades away, a sign that she is low on aura. Tuffdy seizes this opportunity and uses his ability to strangle Momoze in her sleep. 

Momoze's murder is witnessed by Oito, who coincidentally was using Nen to control an insect through the air ducts above the Princes' rooms for reconnaissance at the time. When Sevanti learns of the murder, she immediately accuses the six guards of collusion, and they are taken into custody to face a court-martial.

Marayam Hui Guo Rou

|-|Woble Hui Guo Rou=

Woble Hui Guo Rou

 

Other notable parties

Several non-participating parties still have interest to some degree in this contest, though their specific levels of interest and involvement vary. The eight queens will not be listed since their motivations can largely be condensed down into "helping one or more children survive", neither will any employee or associate of a prince unless they have an interest outside of "helping my prince win".

|Nasubi Hui Guo Rou
|As the current King of Kakin, Nasubi has an obligation to the country to ensure that the succession contest takes place and successfully begins. When Halkenburg confronts Nasubi on Day 6, aiming to use the threat of death to coerce Nasubi into suspending the contest, Nasubi states that the battle for succession is out of his hands, and that there is nothing he can do anymore.
|-
|Kurapika
|Kurapika is hired by Oito as a bodyguard for her daughter Woble. Kurapika applied to Oito's anonymous listing under the mistaken assumption that Halkenburg had been the one to make the listing, but negotiates with her and explains his desire to come into contact with 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou. Oito and Kurapika reach an agreement, and Kurapika agrees to be Woble's bodyguard for the duration of the succession contest.
|-
|Beyond Netero
|Beyond's true interest in the succession contest is grey, but his daughter Longhi strongly believes that he has fathered one of Kakin's fourteen Princes, and likely fathered at least twenty curse sacrifices of varying strengths, with the stronger ones having their curses, activated upon death, targeting Princes. The reasons and evidence for his actions are unclear, but if true, Beyond likely has a very far-reaching plan that involves supporting an unidentified Prince in the battle, even without said Prince necessarily being aware of Beyond or his support.
|}
 

Physical Setting

The succession contest takes place largely on Tier 1 of the Black Whale 1, the highest of its five tiers and also where Kakin's royal family resides for the duration of the empire's voyage to the Dark Continent. Though the contest is not restricted to Tier 1, participants generally remain there for reasons including maintaining social norms and improving their own chances of survival. Leaving the Black Whale 1 itself results in the contest enforcing unspecified rules regarding escape, which are able to kill participants that leave the physical premise of the succession contest. This enforcement comes in the form of ghostly Nen hands that swarm participants that try to escape. Speculated to be the workings of the Lotus Anchorite 

Tier 2 is where Kakin's Justice Bureau is located, and several Princes have been brought there for various reasons, notably 11 th Prince Fugetsu, brought there in the evening of Day 8 after her unsuccessful escape attempt with 10 th Prince Kacho, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 and 9 th Prince Halkenburg, brought there at roughly noon on Day 10 after being arrested by the Justice Bureau under suspicion of premeditated murder targeting 1 st Prince Benjamin's men. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

The Black Whale has a bulkhead separating Tier 2 and Tier 3, only openable from Tier 2 in case of emergencies. As part of the martial law that the Black Whale 1 operates under for the entirety of its journey, the throughways between tiers are guarded by the royal army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Social Setting

Officially, the Princes are equals; the only known official social ranking of the Princes are their titles as "the n th Prince" in order of birth regardless of gender, since gender is not known to have bearing on succession to the throne. Nevertheless, some Princes naturally acquire more social power than others. This is first seen as early as when new of the Succession Contest spreads, reflected through the choice of some Princes to make listings for bodyguards instead of being able to rely on their own connections; 8 th Queen Oito Hui Guo Rou mentions that the higher ranking Princes with power and wealth have personal armies and are well-prepared for the battle, and that the Princes in the weakest positions are relying on blackmailing 9 th Prince Halkenburg's to ensure their safety, with the expectation that his reputation is the one that will suffer the most damage if the knowledge of the succession battle becomes public 

Officially, there is no ranking among Nasubi's eight wives, but the natural order induced by the order they wed Nasubi leads to a strict hierarchy between the wives that makes it difficult for lower Queens to contact higher Queens. While on board the Black Whale, lower Queens are unable to call higher Queens; this is enforced by the military who manages the official phone lines. Speaking broadly, higher Queens are less involved in the succession contest, and lower Queens with less independent children are more involved. The guards of 1 st Queen Unma and 2 nd Queen Duazul are known to be tasked with writing up surveillance reports for the younger Princes through the Queens' Guard system and with a coordinator from Duazul to ensure that Unma's guards do not monopolize the information. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 Unma and Duazul themselves have not been seen doing much throughout the story, this inaction extends to 3 rd and 4 th Queens Tang Zhao Li and Katrono as well. 5 th Queen Swinko-swinko has been seen nagging her only child to participate, and 6 th and 7 th Queens Seiko and Sevanti have been seen taking actions Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 that maximize the chances of one of their children winning the contest. 8 th Queen Oito is almost always beside her child, since Woble is still a baby and unable to be apart from her for any extended period of time.

The Royal Household can assign guards to a Prince. This system reflects their unofficial social hierarchy; whenever a Prince is born, every Queen higher than the newborn's mother can assign one guard to their guard, and so the number of guards a newborn Prince has increases whenever Nasubi officially takes on another wife. Whether the number of guards present in an already-born Prince's guard increases when Nasubi takes on another wife is unconfirmed. In other words, children of the n th Queen have n - 1 guards assigned to them by the Royal Household, one guard from each Queen higher in ranking than the mother of the Prince in question. For example: 
11 th Prince Fugetsu, child of 6 th Queen Seiko, has five spy-guards assigned to her by the Royal Household, one each from Queens 1 through 5 
12 th Prince Momoze, child of 7 th Queen Sevanti, has six spy-guards assigned to her by the Royal Household, one each from Queens 1 through 6 
14 th Prince Woble, child of 8 th Queen Oito, has seven spy-guards assigned to her by the Royal Household, one each from Queens 1 through 7 

This system allows the Queens to use their appointed guards as informants to find out if any lower Queens and Princes are plotting a rebellion. A lower Queen is not allowed to spy on a higher Queen and Prince. 

The Hunter Association restricts each Prince to a maximum of fifteen employees on board. Princes are free to choose how many of their employees are guards and how many are servants, but most Princes chose to have most (if not all) of their employees be guards. This restriction is an upper bound, several Princes have fewer than fifteen employees. Those hired as guards can largely be categorized as Hunters, Royal Bodyguards, and others. Some princes unofficially exceed the limit through methods like having underlings be part of the Royal Army or become Provisional Hunters assigned to a convenient location.

Hunters

Hunters can apply to be bodyguards in several ways. They need not be part of the Hunter Association's mission to be on board; Princes Tyson, Luzurus, Kacho, Momoze, Marayam, and Woble (through her mother Oito) made anonymous listings on Kakin's website to hire bodyguards with preference for Hunters. Pro-Hunters that wish to apply must enter their Hunter Association registration number, and repeat applications are disqualified. Kurapika expects applicants that hide their identities to have their valuations reduced and be assigned far from the Prince's personal detail. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 Applications have only been seen on the one known official Kakin website. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

Because of bodyguards from the Hunter Association that did not appear to know about the nature of the succession contest, Kurapika suspects several Hunters are temp hunters under Pariston's instructions, and that Pariston himself did not know about the nature of the succession contest. 

Provisional Hunters

Because of the Hunter Association's ultimate goal to manage Beyond, whom they believe will do his best to escape upon reaching the Dark Continent, they have a vested interest in reducing the number of allies he can have on board. As such, the Zodiacs plan to let only those necessary for their journey to the dark continent pass the 289 th Hunter Exam. With the addition of Kurapika to the Zodiacs, the Hunter Association weeds out many of Beyond's spies, including everyone from Beyond's Dark Continent Expedition Team; Kurapika warns Mizaistom that he would be unable to weed out those who did not even know they were intended to be Beyond's spies. With the modification to Article 2 of the Hunter Bylaws; participants that pass the 289 th Hunter Examination are titled Provisional Hunters without needing to know Nen; their Hunter Licenses temporary and only valid for the duration of the voyage to the Dark Continent.

Because Provisional Hunters are given their licenses by the Hunter Association with the expectation that they assist with the journey on the Dark Continent after the voyage, they do not necessarily have duties assigned to them by the Hunter Association and are one way that Princes bypass the fifteen employee limit set by the Hunter Association.

Martial Law

For the duration of the voyage, the Black Whale 1 operates under martial law, though martial law has been seen elevated to special martial law. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 There are emergency laws that Kakin still follows during martial law though it is unclear which laws apply to each level of martial law, for example:
Emergency Law Article 1: "Sustaining the Monarchy in a Crisis"; its interpretation leaves room for Benjamin to declare his illegitimate child the successor to the throne, in case of his death while being the lawful successor to the throne. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

It is largely unclear what the full set of rules outlining how Kakin operates under martial law is, but several differences between the various levels of martial law have been mentioned throughout the story:
Martial law　(, jun kaigenrei; lit. Semi-/Quasi-/Equivalent to-Martial Law) In Chapter 358, the original Japanese used is , kaigenrei ni jun, which roughly translates to "a level equivalent to martial law"; the relevant term , jun kaigen in Chapter 380 could mean either equivalent to martial law, which would imply that there has been no difference in martial law-level since the first day, or quasi-martial law, which may also be appropriate given the lockdown imposed the day before. Seeing as there has been no additional rules specified, and a military lockdown of a compound in an area already under martial law does not necessarily need a higher level of martial law, it is possible to treat the two terms as equivalent and there having been no higher level of martial law for the duration of the military's sweep of Tier 3 for the unidentified stowaway. :
The throughways between tiers are guarded by the royal army. 
The passage between Tiers 2 and 3 will be closed, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 and only opened in emergencies from the Tier 2 side. 
Special martial law (, tokushu kaigen; lit. Special Martial Law):
Requires a national survival-level crisis to be declared. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 
Requires a letter of authorization from the King 
Steiner interprets this as the military taking control of the powers of government in the case of a national emergency, and is not corrected by Kaiser. 
All royal army soldiers are granted the right to shoot anybody suspicious without warning. They have the duty to remove any armed citizens and eliminate subversives. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 409 

Events

Kurapika's Nen lessons

In order to increase the chances of a stalemate, Kurapika gives each Prince the opportunity to send up to two guards per round of lessons to learn Nen in two weeks under him. 

 
|-|Round 1=

During the time that the first round of lessons took place, Kurapika additionally has guards Sakata, Hashito, and Slakka from Room 1003 joining them as unofficial participants, and guard Babimyna from Room 1001 joining as an unofficial participant. In Chapter 404, Babimyna confirms that he and Furykov will "again" be the representatives from Room 1001, though Babimyna was not introduced as a participant in the first round of lessons.

|-|Round 2=

8 th Prince Salé-salé had originally planned a rap performance that he believed would "change the world", but would be assassinated before that and his absence attributed to poor health. As a result of his absence, the starting time of the acts in the second half is moved up, but the order did not change. 
 

References
